title: Routing, Selection, Translation, and Redundancy · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: Connect branch, headquarters, cloud, and internet routes while diagnosing asymmetry and failover. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a routed hybrid branch design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.
Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Net Routing Table Forwarding · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network routing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network routing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Net Routing Table Forwarding without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.

content.text: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network routing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Routing Table Forwarding, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a routed hybrid branch design while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Routers choose the route with the lowest metric before comparing prefix length. This fails the Net Routing Table Forwarding contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Subinterfaces applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1Q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.
